text: Test Document
A Comprehensive Test of the DocxCore API

runs.text: A Comprehensive Test of the DocxCore API

text: 1  Introduction

This test document was generated by the DocxCore library to verify that the ParagraphBuilder, TableBuilder, DocumentWriter, SectionBuilder, and StyleBuilder classes work correctly together. The library provides a fluent API for creating Word documents programmatically from .NET code.
2  Features Overview
2.1  ParagraphBuilder

The ParagraphBuilder class provides a fluent interface for creating styled paragraphs. It supports text, bold text, superscript text, alignment, indentation, line spacing, and page break controls. Paragraphs can be composed by chaining method calls in a builder pattern.
Note: This paragraph was built directly with ParagraphBuilder and appended to the body, bypassing DocumentWriter.

runs.text: This paragraph was built directly with ParagraphBuilder and appended to the body, bypassing DocumentWriter.

text: 2.2  TableBuilder

The TableBuilder class creates tables with customizable columns, header rows, data rows, border styles (including three-line academic table format), and Word built-in table styles. It uses a fluent API similar to ParagraphBuilder.
2.3  DocumentWriter

The DocumentWriter class provides a high-level API for building complete academic documents. It supports automatic heading numbering, reference detection in body text (e.g., [1]), footnotes, endnotes, cross-references, tables of contents, and more.
2.4  Sample Data Table (TableBuilder)
The following table was created using the TableBuilder class with three columns and three data rows. It demonstrates the fluent table-building API.
2.5  Three-Line Academic Table (TableBuilder)
The following table uses the ThreeLineBorders method to create a standard academic three-line table format.
2.6  Footnotes and Endnotes
This paragraph demonstrates footnote functionality
and this sentence is followed by an endnote
. The references [1] should be automatically detected and formatted as superscript.
2.7  Cross References
Cross references allow linking to bookmarked locations within the document. For example,
see this bookmarked section
demonstrates cross-reference functionality.
3  References
References
[1] Smith, J. (2024). Document Generation Techniques for Scientific Computing. Journal of Software Engineering, 15(3), 123-145.
